Interface IInputEditor

Describes a base interface for input editors.

The IInputEditor interface is implemented by the UI library and is not intended to be implemented by consumer code.

Inherited Members
ISynchronizeInvoke.BeginInvoke(Delegate, object[])
ISynchronizeInvoke.EndInvoke(IAsyncResult)
ISynchronizeInvoke.Invoke(Delegate, object[])
ISynchronizeInvoke.InvokeRequired
IDisposable.IsDisposed
IComponent.Site
IComponent.Disposed
IDisposable.Dispose()
IWin32Window.Handle
Namespace: PhiRobotics.UI.Editors
Assembly: PhiRobotics.UI.dll
Syntax
public interface IInputEditor : ISynchronizeInvoke, IDisposable, IComponent, IDisposable, IWin32Window
Remarks

The IInputEditor interface defines common functionality for all editor controls that allow users to view and modify values.

Editors expose their value through the EditValue property and provide events to track value changes before and after they occur.

In addition to value handling, this interface provides support for validation feedback, error indication, and tooltip integration.

Instances of IInputEditor are created using the Create(InputEditorId) method or dedicated factory methods for creating specific editor types.

The lifetime of an IInputEditor instance is managed by the consumer. Consumers are responsible for disposing the editor when it is no longer needed.

Examples

The following example demonstrates how to create a text editor:

IInputEditor editor = inputEditorFactory.Create(InputEditorId.TextEdit);
ITextInputEditor textEditor = (ITextInputEditor)editor;

Properties

EditValue

Gets or sets the editor’s value.

Declaration
object EditValue { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
object

An object representing the editor’s value.
Remarks

Setting this property raises the EditValueChanging event. After the value has been updated, the EditValueChanged event is raised.

ErrorIconAlignment

Gets or sets the alignment of the error icon displayed for the editor.

Declaration
ErrorIconAlignment ErrorIconAlignment { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
ErrorIconAlignment

An ErrorIconAlignment value that specifies the error icon alignment.

ErrorText

Gets or sets the tooltip displayed when the mouse pointer hovers over the error icon.

Declaration
string ErrorText { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
string

A string value that specifies the error tooltip text.

RichToolTip

Gets or sets a rich tooltip associated with the editor.

Declaration
IRichToolTip RichToolTip { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
IRichToolTip

An IRichToolTip instance that defines the tooltip content.
Remarks

Use the CreateToolTip() method to create a rich tooltip instance.

ShowToolTips

Gets or sets whether tooltips are displayed for the editor.

Declaration
bool ShowToolTips { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
bool

true to display tooltips; otherwise, false.

ToolTipController

Gets or sets the tooltip controller that manages tooltip behavior for the editor.

Declaration
IToolTipController ToolTipController { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
IToolTipController

An IToolTipController instance that controls tooltip appearance and behavior.
Remarks

Use the RichToolTip property to assign tooltip content.

Events

EditValueChanged

Raised immediately after the EditValue has changed.

Declaration
event EventHandler EditValueChanged
Event Type
Type Description
EventHandler

EditValueChanging

Raised when the editor’s value is about to change.

Declaration
event EventHandler<InputChangingEventArgs> EditValueChanging
Event Type
Type Description
EventHandler<InputChangingEventArgs>
Remarks

The event data is provided by InputChangingEventArgs, which exposes the following properties:

  • OldValueGets the current editor value.
  • NewValue Gets or sets the value that is about to be assigned to the editor. Setting this property is not supported when the editor uses masked input.
note

Avoid displaying modal dialogs or changing focus within this event, as it may cause the editor to lose its current value.
